description: Seed registry for public and public-interest Flint civic data sources. Entries describe source trust, privacy exposure, first ingestion targets, and review needs before data is made public.

sources.name: City of Flint GIS
sources.steward: City of Flint GIS / Planning Division
known_limits: Portal availability and download formats need to be probed from live services.
Official geometry can still be stale or incomplete for current field conditions.
first_checks: Identify ArcGIS REST service endpoints and downloadable layer formats.
Record source update timestamps per layer.
Compare boundary geometries against UM-Flint and Genesee County references.

sources.name: University of Michigan-Flint GIS Center Data
sources.steward: University of Michigan-Flint GIS Center
sources.geography: Flint, Genesee County, and seven-county I-69 Thumb Corridor
known_limits: Some files require contacting the GIS Center instead of direct download.
Census attributes and geometries may have different vintages.
first_checks: Create a request list for files that are not direct downloads.
Separate geometry vintages from ACS metric vintages.
Document which fields are source-derived versus atlas-derived.

sources.name: Flint Property Portal
sources.steward: Genesee County Land Bank Authority and City of Flint origin partnership
known_limits: Current update reliability is uncertain.
Property data can imply household-level conditions and must be handled carefully.
Use as an origin story and comparison source before treating as a canonical feed.
first_checks: Review terms, robots, and available export/API paths.
Record latest observable update timestamps.
Limit early public display to source freshness and aggregate patterns unless reuse is clearly allowed.

sources.name: MapFlint
sources.steward: MapFlint / UM-Flint affiliated data products
known_limits: Some map layers are older analytical products, not live operational feeds.
Download links may live on ArcGIS open-data subdomains.
first_checks: Inventory each category and its source vintage.
Prioritize health resources, parks, and economic indicators for v0.1.
Label older products as historical context instead of current fact.

sources.name: Michigan Traffic Crash Facts
sources.steward: Michigan Traffic Crash Facts / Michigan State Police release cycle
known_limits: Crash location precision and export permissions need verification.
Annual official closed datasets may lag current conditions.
first_checks: Confirm Flint and Genesee County query export paths.
Normalize annual crash counts by tract, ward, road segment, and corridor where legally usable.
Preserve crash statistics as aggregate safety indicators, not person-level records.

sources.name: CDC PLACES Census Tract Data 2025 Release
sources.steward: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
known_limits: Tract estimates are modeled public health measures, not clinical records.
Uncertainty and measure definitions must be visible in the atlas.
first_checks: Filter to Flint tracts by GEOID.
Attach measure metadata and confidence/limitation notes.
Avoid ranking neighborhoods without plain-language caveats.

sources.name: U.S. Census ACS and TIGER/Line
sources.steward: U.S. Census Bureau
known_limits: TIGER/Line provides geography, while ACS provides estimates and margins of error.
ACS estimates require margin-of-error handling before comparison views.
first_checks: Pin a starting ACS 5-year vintage.
Store margins of error with every ACS metric.
Use GEOID as the stable join key.

sources.name: Genesee County GIS
sources.steward: Genesee County
known_limits: County parcel and ownership context may expose personal data and should not be mirrored casually.
Source terms and field-level privacy policy need review before public export.
first_checks: Identify public GIS endpoints and terms.
Separate public geometry from sensitive ownership or assessment fields.
Use this as a reconciliation source for official boundaries before parcel dossiers.

sources.name: Library of Congress Sanborn Fire Insurance Maps for Flint
sources.steward: Library of Congress Geography and Map Division
known_limits: Sanborn maps are historical snapshots and should not be treated as current parcel truth.
Georeferencing, sheet stitching, and OCR extraction require manual review before public geometry claims.
first_checks: Capture Flint volume years and sheet identifiers relevant to Lost Flint research.
Record IIIF or image access paths and georeferencing assumptions.
Use map-derived building presence as reviewable historical evidence, not automatic demolition facts.

sources.name: HathiTrust Flint City Directories
sources.steward: HathiTrust Digital Library
known_limits: Historical directories can contain personally identifying names, addresses, and occupations even when records are old.
Some volumes are view-restricted or rights-limited and may require manual citation instead of bulk text extraction.
first_checks: Inventory Flint directory years and stable volume links.
Decide a public-safe excerpt policy for names and address listings.
Treat directory-derived occupancy as historical evidence for Lost Flint review, not as a standalone truth source.

sources.name: Genesee Historical Aerial Imagery
sources.steward: Historic Aerials / NETR Online with Michigan imagery context from MiSAIL
sources.geography: Genesee County and City of Flint
known_limits: Historic imagery licensing and export rights may differ from simple on-screen viewing rights.
Aerial interpretation can imply false precision when exact demolition or build dates are unknown.
first_checks: Record which Flint and Genesee years are publicly visible without account-gated export.
Separate visual review use from any downloadable raster workflow.
Use historical aerials as corroborating evidence for presence intervals and site change review.

sources.name: Genesee County Land Bank Demolition Program
sources.steward: Genesee County Land Bank Authority
known_limits: Address-level demolition records can imply household-level conditions and must be handled carefully.
Program schedules change as properties are sold, cancelled, or delayed.
first_checks: Capture stable list or export paths for demolition schedules and status fields.
Separate Flint-specific demolitions from countywide lists.
Show demolition activity as reviewable source-backed interventions, not as a complete building history.

sources.name: RACER Trust Buick City Site
sources.steward: Revitalizing Auto Communities Environmental Response Trust
known_limits: RACER materials focus on site status and reuse, not a complete historical chronology of Buick City.
Property narratives may require cross-checking with local archives before they are presented as civic history.
first_checks: Capture any downloadable site maps or public property documents.
Record site acreage, reuse milestones, and public status language.
Link RACER site history to Lost Flint building/site records only through cited review notes.

sources.name: OpenHistoricalMap
sources.steward: OpenHistoricalMap / OpenStreetMap US community
known_limits: Community-edited historical data needs visible provenance and should not outrank official or archival source evidence by default.
Temporal tagging quality can vary by feature and contributor.
first_checks: Identify Flint-area query paths or export formats from the OHM API.
Record license and attribution rules alongside any imported features.
Use OHM as corroborating context for Lost Flint and site dossiers, not as the only source.

sources.name: National Register of Historic Places Spatial Data
sources.steward: National Park Service Cultural Resources GIS Facility
sources.geography: United States, filterable to Flint and Genesee County
known_limits: NPS warns that some points and polygon boundaries need correction and should not be treated as field-verified geometry.
Sensitive or secure geographies are omitted from the public dataset.
first_checks: Filter unrestricted listings to Flint and Genesee County.
Store National Register identifiers and listing dates with any public feature.
Use the dataset as a historic-place anchor and corroboration source, not as a full building inventory.

sources.name: City of Flint Capital Improvement Plan
sources.steward: City of Flint Planning Department
known_limits: The document itself warns that some cost, funding, or status details may be out of date as allocations change.
Project descriptions need place resolution and source-date labeling before public map use.
first_checks: Extract project names, departments, statuses, and funding fields into intervention candidates.
Geocode only reviewed place references, not every free-text description automatically.
Present CIP items as planned or underway interventions, not completed outcomes unless another source confirms them.

sources.name: City of Flint Community Services and HUD Action Plans
sources.steward: City of Flint Community Services Department
known_limits: Program pages and action plans describe allocations and intended uses, not verified physical outcomes.
Some linked PDFs can change between annual funding cycles and need date-specific citations.
first_checks: Capture the latest action-plan and allocation documents with dates.
Separate grant categories, implementing organizations, and target geographies.
Represent HUD-related work as intervention ledger records with explicit source dates and stages.

sources.name: Flint Water Service Line Materials Map
sources.steward: Flint service line map collaborators using ongoing City of Flint data
sources.geography: City of Flint residential service lines
known_limits: The public map includes address-level infrastructure context and modeled probabilities that require careful public-use boundaries.
The site terms limit use and explicitly warn that the map should not be used as a legal or guaranteed accuracy source.
first_checks: Capture terms-of-use language and whether bulk export is allowed.
Decide whether public atlas views must stay aggregated rather than address-level.
Treat modeled service-line predictions as uncertainty-bearing observations, not definitive infrastructure facts.

sources.name: Michigan Geographic Framework Roads
sources.steward: Michigan DTMB Center for Shared Solutions
sources.geography: Michigan statewide roads, filterable to Flint and Genesee County
known_limits: Statewide framework roads are strong basemap references, but local lane or curb conditions may still differ on the ground.
The atlas should clip to Flint/Genesee scope and preserve source vintage.
first_checks: Identify the exact open-data road centerline layer used for Flint corridor analysis.
Record functional class and jurisdiction fields relevant to street-safety work.
Use MGF roads as the spatial skeleton for crash and intervention rollups.

sources.name: MDOT Traffic Monitoring Program
sources.steward: Michigan Department of Transportation
sources.geography: Michigan statewide traffic counts and AADT references
known_limits: ADT and AADT are aggregated traffic estimates and need method labeling for short-count versus continuous-count sources.
The public workflow emphasizes dashboards and program pages, so export reproducibility needs to be documented.
first_checks: Record Flint and Genesee count retrieval steps from the public program surface.
Decide which traffic metrics belong in first public corridor cards.
Cross-link corridor and road volumes to Michigan Traffic Crash Facts aggregate safety records.

sources.name: Genesee County Metropolitan Planning Commission Traffic Counts
sources.steward: Genesee County Metropolitan Planning Commission with partner agencies
sources.geography: Flint and Genesee County road network
known_limits: The public evidence currently found is map/PDF oriented and may be stale for current traffic conditions.
Traffic-count maps summarize local agency observations but are not yet a clean machine-readable feed.
first_checks: Find the newest public Flint/Genesee count maps or tables from the planning commission or successor surface.
Document agency provenance for local counts versus MDOT trunkline counts.
Use this source as local corridor context and compare it with MDOT traffic and crash aggregates.

sources.name: Flint MTA GTFS
sources.steward: Mass Transportation Authority of Flint
sources.geography: Flint and Genesee County transit network
known_limits: GTFS is excellent for scheduled service geometry and timing, but does not prove real-time performance or accessibility quality by itself.
Developer terms and feed-hosting arrangements need to be captured before mirroring.
first_checks: Confirm the current static GTFS ZIP path and licensing terms.
Clip transit coverage and stop access to Flint-focused mobility views.
Use GTFS to build route and stop context for corridor dossiers, not to imply live service reliability.

sources.name: Transitland Flint MTA GTFS Mirror
sources.steward: Transitland / Interline feed registry
known_limits: Transitland is a useful mirror and metadata registry, but upstream agency terms still govern real use.
Mirror availability can lag or differ from the agency-hosted GTFS feed.
first_checks: Record the upstream GTFS URL, last fetch date, and license metadata from Transitland.
Prefer the agency feed for canonical schedule ingestion and use Transitland as a discoverability backstop.
Use Transitland metadata to detect feed freshness drift or hosting changes.
